sections.paragraph:
plur. f. (from the root הָתַל) deceitful things, Isa 30:10.

I. מֹו properly i.q. מָה what, (that) which, pleon. joined in poetic language to the prepositions בְּ, כְּ, לְ, so that there are formed the separate words בְּמֹו, כְּמֹו, לְמֹו, like بِمَا for بِ, كَمَا for كَ, عَمَّا for عَنْ, see de Sacy, Gr. Arabe, i. § 824, 826, 839; ii. § 82, and Lehrgeb. page 629. As to כְּמֹו and לְמֹו see in their respective places בְּמֹו poetically used for in, Psa 11:2 Isa 25:10 43:2 44:16 into, Job 37:8 through, of the instrument, Job 16:4, Job 16:5.

II. מֹו i.q. מַי Arab. مَاءُ water (see מַי ), from the unused root מֹוא. [See Job 9:30 (כתיב); see more in Thes.]
